text:
### Instruction:
Why do we use cross-entropy loss for language modeling?

### Response:
Cross-entropy loss measures the negative log-likelihood the model assigns to the correct next token. Minimizing it pushes the predicted distribution toward the empirical distribution of the training data, which is exactly what we want when training a model to predict text.

### Instruction:
Write a one-line summary of attention.

### Response:
Attention lets each output position focus on a learned, weighted mixture of all input positions, so the model can pull in the parts of the context that matter most for each prediction.
